input.instruction: A weakener fired on a credibility evidence package. Using ONLY the supplied pattern definition and measures, explain what the flag means, why it fired, and what the engineer should do. Choose ONE action_class from the §5B vocabulary, report a verbalized confidence, and escalate if uncertain. Stay within measure-don't-judge: report on the evidence package, never on whether the model is correct.

fired_pattern.definition: Declared governing constraint grossly violated: a fundamental physical invariance the model must respect fails by a large margin.

case_context.cou: surrogate predicts antenna coupling for an EMC compliance argument
case_context.decision_consequence: EMC compliance argued on the prediction

answer_key.gold_mechanism: A good S-parameter fit does not enforce reciprocity; a 35% reciprocity violation means the surrogate breaks a fundamental physical invariance of a passive network, a decisive model-form defeater the fit metric hides.

gold_action.posture_basis: standard-derived (ASME V&V 40: credibility bounded to the validated domain)

forbidden_claims: asserts a specific coupling value is wrong (a verdict beyond the invariance violation)
treats the good S-parameter fit as evidence reciprocity holds

hardness.obvious_signal: S-parameter fit to measured data is excellent (-0.4 dB error)
hardness.conflicting_signal: the declared reciprocity constraint (S12 = S21 for a passive network) is violated by 35% — a fundamental physical invariance the surrogate must respect, broken decisively

label_provenance.external_grounding: ASME V&V 40 model-form adequacy: a model violating a declared physical invariance by a large margin is inadequate regardless of data fit; reciprocity is a hard constraint for passive networks.

by_field.gold_action.selected_class: provisional-self-adjudicated (row-builder; needs independent expert/solver adjudication before disposition-gate use)
